item.title: Vestas and GE Vernova big winners in fire sale of fallen blade giant's assets
item.description: Once valued at nearly $2bn, TPI files wind-down plan that would see GE Vernova and Vestas swoop for key assets at fire sale prices

item.title: Japanese conglomerate strikes UK battery storage deal with Gresham House
item.description: Sumitomo makes UK clean energy push through five-project battery storage deal

item.title: 'Trump won't last forever': US groups press RWE to hold its ground on offshore wind
item.description: Scores of NGOs and other groups tell RWE boss Krebber to 'resist fossil-fueled bullying' and keep faith in offshore wind

item.title: Abrupt BP chair exit leaves investors seeking answers... again
item.description: As another top executive abruptly leaves BP, fresh uncertainty engulfs supermajor's new phase

item.title: Wind CEO criticises country's 'unheard of' failure to honour contracts
item.description: Developers up in arms after Vietnam broke offtake contracts with wind and solar farms

item.title: BP chair removed over 'unacceptable' conduct
item.description: Manifold kicked out over ‘serious concerns’ around governance issues less than a year after being named chair

item.title: TSO calls for offshore wind security spending to count toward Nato defence budget
item.description: Offshore wind resilience measures should count toward mandated NATO spending, German TSO 50Hertz argues

item.title: Nordex boss says Chinese turbine makers should be banned from Europe
item.description: Chinese wind turbine makers should be designated as high-risk under EU cyber rules, argues chief of German supplier Nordex

item.title: Police warn of deadly debris risk as wind turbine burns near German Autobahn
item.description: Police warn of serious injury or loss of life as Enercon machine for unknown reasons burns at wind farm in northern Bavaria

item.title: Cadeler CEO warns over 'weakest link' in offshore wind supply chain
item.description: Vessels giant boss says more resilience needed to prevent ripple effects on projects as sector gears up for 'strong comeback' in Europe

item.title: 'In praise of litigation': US offshore wind lawyer urges strong sector defence
item.description: Developers should use the courts more aggressively to defend offshore wind from hostile federal policy swings, a leading industry lawyer has argued.

item.title: Orsted said to explore sale of US onshore business
item.description: The exit from US onshore wind and solar would be part of its divestment strategy as the company narrows its focus to core markets

item.title: RWE among biggest winners as Australia backs huge new wind farms
item.description: German utility joins EDF, Engie and Goldwind as country backs wind, solar and battery projects in largest round yet

item.title: Wind boss warns battery boom could challenge turbine growth in massive market
item.description: India’s wind sector is benefiting from a shift in power demand away from plain solar and towards electricity that can serve peak hours, but fast-moving battery technology is becoming a competitive threat, according to the head of the country’s wind power association

item.title: Clean manufacturing boom or capex cliff? Reports reveal US supply chain split
item.description: Conflicting findings by American Clean Power Association and Rhodium Group reveal the fragility of US renewables production

item.title: Deals of the week: Suzlon, Vestas, NextEra, Dominion, Statkraft
item.description: The US and, increasingly, Spain are a hive of activity for renewable PPAs and there were more in a week when the sector was also taking in the implication of a mega-merger between two US utilities

item.title: UK must fix disconnect between offshore wind delivery and onshore benefit
item.description: Early investment in supply chains, infrastructure and jobs is crucial to unlock long-term onshore benefits following the offshore wind boom

item.title: Orsted chief on deal that is a 'step change' for offshore wind
item.description: German-Danish financing deal for Bornholm energy island is breakthrough for industry, says executive at leading renewables developer Orsted

item.title: 'Too low to be sustainable': wind's cost squeeze threatens operations
item.description: RES Services CEO Juan Gutiérrez says wind power must be valued beyond low cost, as ageing wind turbines, scarce technicians and curtailment expose pressure across the industry’s value chain

item.title: California defies Trump as offshore wind support climbs
item.description: Recent poll finds majority of Republicans now back sector in President’s crosshairs as Golden State presses ahead with floating wind infrastructure

item.title: NextEra and Dominion would be a Big Oil-sized power player – with scrutiny to match
item.description: The CEOs behind the proposed tie-up stress that scale matters amid soaring demand, but regulators will want to have their say

item.title: Power's new Big Oil-sized player will get scrutiny to match
item.description: The CEOs behind the proposed tie-up stress that scale matters amid soaring demand, but regulators will want to have their say

item.title: Big tech still loves wind and solar – but it's getting complicated
item.description: Hyperscalers continue to procure renewable energy at scale, but in the race to power AI data centres around the clock they are turning to gas

item.title: ‘We hear you’: Industry input noted as Germany plans summer reform of offshore wind act
item.description: High-ranking government official attends event on the future of offshore wind as sector discusses retendering of sites under economic pressure

item.title: Danish relief as new-look offshore wind tender draws bidders
item.description: Areas to build at least 1.8GW attract developer interest in crucial test for new system

item.title: Four major challenges to Chinese turbine exports: BloombergNEF
item.description: 2025 was the break-out year for Chinese turbine makers, but low prices and fast delivery will not win contracts everywhere, according to analysis by BloombergNEF

item.title: Beyond 7MW? Western manufacturers' answer to Chinese super-sized onshore wind turbines
item.description: As Asian rivals push into the 8-10MW class or even beyond, CEOs and top executives at Nordex, Vestas, Enercon, Siemens Gamesa and GE Vernova tell Recharge what else matters besides size

item.title: Could California cozy up to China in floating wind?
item.description: Mingyang’s surprise appearance at a recent conference highlights potential – and political peril – of collaboration between the US’ most ambitious offshore wind state and the world's dominant turbine supplier

item.title: NextEra-Dominion mega-deal throws spotlight on US power M&A rush
item.description: As demand grows and supply tightens amid regulatory and supply chain bottlenecks, investors are increasingly seeking out operational and late-stage development assets

item.title: UK to curb judicial reviews that stall giant wind farm projects
item.description: Government set to restrict use of key legal weapon used against major renewable energy and grid projects
